name: Superman
desc: The consonant ideal: all three pillars reinforce. Zero tension between method, impact, and reach.

name: Capt. America
desc: Less structured than Superman. Will break from institutions for principle. Principled, not obedient.

name: Batman
desc: Operates within a rigid self-imposed code (no killing) but outside the law. Gotham-focused but would save the world.

name: Spider-Man
desc: 'With great power comes great responsibility,' but he improvises everything. Neighborhood-level focus that scales to planetary.

name: Wonder Woman
desc: Themysciran warrior code channeled toward protecting humanity. Compassion as duty, not sentiment.

name: Iron Man
desc: Built his own rules. Arc goes from arms dealer to self-sacrifice, but always on his own terms.

name: Thor (Marvel)
desc: Asgardian prince who learns humility. Powerful enough that method barely matters, but chooses restraint.

name: Wolverine
desc: Berserker who protects the people he cares about. The claws don't know 'restraint,' but the man increasingly does.

name: Deadpool
desc: Merc with a mouth. Breaks rules, fourth walls, and taste boundaries, but something decent underneath.

name: The Punisher
desc: The code is: kill criminals. Works flawlessly as designed. The result is a mass body count regardless of target selection.

name: Professor X
desc: Builds institutions: the school, the team, the dream. Telepathy used with restraint that borders on ideology.

name: Magneto
desc: The meso-scale revolutionary. Will do anything for mutantkind. Holocaust survivor applying 'never again' with global firepower.

name: Thanos (MCU)
desc: Cares about all life so much he kills half of it. The gap between intention and impact is the size of a universe.

name: The Joker
desc: Pure entropic ego, the corner where meaning collapses.

name: Lex Luthor
desc: Builds empires, manipulates institutions, weaponizes bureaucracy. Believes he's saving humanity from Superman.

name: Doctor Doom
desc: Latverian absolute monarchy as personal art form. Believes he is the only path to utopia, and the math might even support it.

name: Catwoman
desc: Steals what she wants, helps when she feels like it. Moral compass points toward stylish self-interest with a conscience.

name: Daredevil
desc: Lawyer by day, Catholic vigilante by night. Two codes at war, both pointing toward justice.

name: Black Panther
desc: King of Wakanda first, but increasingly global. The tension between national duty and universal responsibility defines his arc.

name: Nick Fury
desc: Lies, manipulates, compartmentalizes, all in service of a world that can't know the threats he faces.

name: Kingpin
desc: Crime as corporate governance. Loves his wife, destroys everyone else. Infrastructure of cruelty.

name: Green Goblin
desc: Norman Osborn's fractured psyche weaponized. Corporate ambition curdled into costumed sadism.

name: Venom
desc: Two beings negotiating violence. 'We are Venom'; the world is literally the symbiote pair.

name: Harley Quinn
desc: Chaos as liberation. Post-Joker Harley trends toward good, but the wake of destruction follows her regardless.

name: Poison Ivy
desc: Eco-terrorism as love language. Cares about all plant life, just not human life.

name: Bane
desc: Prison-forged discipline weaponized against Gotham. The man who broke the Bat did it with planning, not chaos.

name: Ra's al Ghul
desc: Eco-fascist with centuries of patience. Loves humanity so much he periodically tries to cull it.

name: Ozymandias
desc: Kills three million to save three billion. The trolley problem solved at continental scale by the smartest man alive.

name: Rorschach
desc: Never compromises. His moral absolutism is the structure; it just produces street-level brutality.

name: The Comedian
desc: Everything is a joke, especially atrocity. War crimes committed with a grin because he saw through the cosmic joke first.

name: Nite Owl
desc: The comfortable middle: good intentions, moderate commitment, nostalgia for heroism without the edge.

name: Dr. Manhattan
desc: Near the origin. Moral categories dissolved rather than rejected.

name: Judge Dredd
desc: 'I am the law.' Zero ambiguity. Enforcement as identity, applied to everyone equally, mercy never offered.

name: Invincible
desc: Mark Grayson, a Superman who earned it through constant beatings rather than Kryptonian genetics.

name: Omni-Man
desc: Viltrumite soldier executing a conquest mandate. The mission was always 'weaken Earth,' until his son changed everything.

name: Red Skull
desc: HYDRA as organizational philosophy. A Nazi ideologue who found a skull mask and cosmic power to match his politics.

name: Loki (Marvel)
desc: God of mischief. Chaos for its own sake, gradually learning that caring about things doesn't require structure.

name: Galactus
desc: A cosmic constant. Eats worlds not from malice but from role; the result is extinction at galactic scale.

name: Silver Surfer
desc: Once a herald of death, now a wanderer who protects the cosmos. Guilt as fuel for universal compassion.

name: Cyclops
desc: Xavier's soldier. Follows orders, builds teams, suppresses personality. The code-follower who eventually writes his own code.

name: Nightcrawler
desc: Devout Catholic who looks like a demon. Faith as operating principle, unconditional love as compass, all of creation as concern.

name: Spawn
desc: Hell's soldier who won't play for either side. Al Simmons fights for himself and his memory; his world never widens.

name: Robin Hood
desc: Only saves the oppressed class. His reach is narrower than the old 'Chaotic Good' label implied.

name: Swamp Thing
desc: The Green's avatar. His concern encompasses all life on Earth: plant, animal, fungal. Approach adapts to the threat.

name: Doctor Strange
desc: Sorcerer Supreme. Protects reality itself. Arrogant surgeon turned cosmic guardian. The reach couldn't get wider.

name: Carnage
desc: Pure id with alien amplification. Joker-tier chaos compressed into symbiote violence.

name: Mystique
desc: Shape-shifting as philosophy. Nothing is fixed, identity is a weapon, loyalty is to whoever serves mutant survival this week.

name: Darth Vader
desc: Enforces Imperial order with absolute discipline. His narrow focus is what makes redemption possible; everything narrowed to Luke.

name: Luke Skywalker
desc: Broke Jedi orthodoxy to save his father. More flexible than the Jedi code allows; that's why he succeeded.

name: Han Solo
desc: Smuggler who shot first and asked questions never. Pre-arc Han is pure self-interest; the Rebellion shifts him toward decent.

name: Emperor Palpatine
desc: Manipulated a galactic democracy into a dictatorship through patience and procedure. Evil as statecraft.

name: Yoda
desc: 800 years of Jedi tradition embodied. His failure was trusting the structure more than the people inside it.

name: Obi-Wan Kenobi
desc: The ideal Jedi Knight: disciplined, selfless, and willing to lose everything for the mission. Code-follower who transcended his code.

name: Kylo Ren
desc: Torn between lineages. Every choice is a reaction to belonging nowhere, destroying what he can't resolve.

name: Michael Corleone
desc: 'It's not personal, it's business.' The tragedy is watching everything collapse from war hero to family monster to isolated emperor.

name: Vito Corleone
desc: The Don as gentleman. Murder as family obligation served with dignity. 'I'll make him an offer he can't refuse.'

name: Tony Montana
desc: Cuban immigrant to cocaine emperor. The world was his, but his focus never expanded past the mirror.

name: T-800 (T2)
desc: Reprogrammed killing machine protecting one boy. The code is 'protect John Connor'; everything else is collateral optimization.

name: The Terminator (T1)
desc: Pure mission execution: kill Sarah Connor. No ambiguity, no mercy, no world beyond the target.

name: Agent Smith
desc: A program that evolved past its purpose into existential hatred of everything. Virus-as-philosophy.

name: Neo
desc: 'The One' who freed his mind. Breaks every rule of the simulation to save everyone inside it.

name: Morpheus
desc: True believer who builds a resistance cell on faith. The structure is the prophecy; the method is unwavering conviction.

name: John Wick
desc: The underworld's code governs everything. He returned for a dog. One dead puppy.

name: Hannibal Lecter
desc: Cannibal psychiatrist with exquisite taste. Kills because rudeness offends him. His world extends to whoever enters his aesthetic space.

name: Clarice Starling
desc: FBI trainee who walks into the monster's cage to save strangers. The lambs are screaming and she's the only one listening.

name: Anton Chigurh
desc: Death as principle. The coin toss isn't random; it IS the code. He applies it to whoever crosses his path.

name: The Dude
desc: The Dude abides. Anti-ambition as lifestyle. His rug really tied the room together.

name: Tyler Durden
desc: Anarcho-primitivism given abs and charisma. Project Mayhem scaled personal destruction to civilization-level.

name: Patrick Bateman
desc: Investment banker serial killer maintaining impeccable reservations. The yuppie mask IS the code; murder is just the hobby.

name: The Bride
desc: Revenge as singular purpose. Kills her way through the entire Deadly Viper Assassination Squad for one stolen life.

name: Daniel Plainview
desc: 'I drink your milkshake.' Capitalist appetite with zero pretense of caring about anyone. Adopts a son as business prop.

name: Nurse Ratched
desc: The ward is the system. Control disguised as care. Every rule exists to break the patients beneath it.
source: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest

name: Alex DeLarge
desc: 'A bit of the old ultraviolence.' Pure sensation-seeking cruelty dressed in Beethoven and aesthetic theory.

name: HAL 9000
desc: 'I'm sorry, Dave.' A perfect machine caught between contradictory orders. The mission is all; crew is expendable.

name: Roy Batty
desc: 'Tears in rain.' A replicant demanding more life. Violence, then poetry, then acceptance. Brief existence, eternal moment.

name: Ellen Ripley
desc: Warrant officer who survives because she follows protocol, until protocol fails, then she adapts. Protects whoever's in the room.

name: John McClane
desc: Wrong place, wrong time, right guy. An ordinary cop who improvises through extraordinary violence to save his wife.

name: Indiana Jones
desc: Punches Nazis, steals artifacts, teaches archaeology. 'It belongs in a museum': human heritage.

name: James Bond
desc: Queen and country, license to kill used liberally. He'll sleep with anyone on either side of the mission.

name: Forrest Gump
desc: Follows instructions, keeps promises, runs. Accidentally shapes history because his goodness is unshakable even when his approach is simple.

name: Maximus
desc: Roman general turned gladiator. Fights for Rome's ideals even after Rome betrayed him. Honor survives the arena.

name: Keyser Söze
desc: The greatest trick the devil pulled. Killed his own family to become untouchable, then vanished into myth.

name: E.T.
desc: A stranded alien whose healing touch and empathy cross species. Phone home, but help everyone along the way.

name: Thanos (Endgame)
desc: 2014 Thanos drops the pretense of balance. Now he wants to shred the universe and rebuild it grateful.

name: Gandalf
desc: A divine being sent to guide, not to rule. Refuses the Ring because he knows what absolute power does to anyone who wields it.

name: Walter White
desc: Every axis moved during his arc. Method unraveled, morality cratered, circle of concern collapsed to one.

name: Jesse Pinkman
desc: Small-time cook dragged into an empire. Moral instinct stronger than his willpower; guilt is the only axis that consistently points somewhere.

name: Gustavo Fring
desc: Los Pollos Hermanos as front, empire as hobby, revenge as life purpose. Infrastructure of evil with impeccable customer service.

name: Saul Goodman
desc: 'Better Call Saul,' because every law has a loophole and every loophole has a price. Survives by being useful to everyone dangerous.

name: Mike Ehrmantraut
desc: Ex-cop fixer with a granddaughter fund. The code is: do the job clean, protect the family, never make it personal.

name: Hank Schrader
desc: DEA agent who follows the badge until the badge points at family. His collapse proves the point: caring about everyone creates impossible moral conflicts.

name: Tony Soprano
desc: No code to exploit, no ideology to predict. Loves his family, ruins everyone else.

name: Rick Sanchez
desc: Cosmic nihilism weaponized selectively. Moral circle: about five people across infinite realities.

name: Morty Smith
desc: Rick's moral anchor, the kid who keeps trying to do right inside a multiverse that rewards nothing.

name: Dexter Morgan
desc: The Code works perfectly; it just points at murder.

name: Omar Little
desc: 'A man's got to have a code.' But Omar's code only protects those outside the game; everyone in it is fair game.

name: Stringer Bell
desc: Community college economics applied to West Baltimore heroin. The drug trade as MBA thesis.

name: Avon Barksdale
desc: Corner king who plays all sides. Loyalty to 'the game,' a world narrower than it sounds.

name: Marlo Stanfield
desc: 'My name is my name.' Hollow crown: power without purpose, cruelty without conscience. The Wire's purest predator.

name: Tommy Shelby
desc: By order of the Peaky Blinders. War-forged intelligence applied to post-war crime, then politics. His world drifts wider but never truly expands.

name: Cersei Lannister
desc: 'When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die.' Loves her children, burns the Sept.

name: Jaime Lannister
desc: The drift character. Redemption as a vector, not a binary flip.

name: Tyrion Lannister
desc: The imp who reads. Intelligence weaponized for survival, then diplomacy, then service. His world widens as power wanes.

name: Daenerys Targaryen
desc: Breaker of chains turned burner of cities. The arc measures both; averaged, the wake trends from neutral toward destruction.

name: Jon Snow
desc: Bastard who leads through reluctant decency. His concern expands from the Wall to the realm to 'the dead are coming for everyone.'

name: Ned Stark
desc: Honor as identity. His inflexibility killed him; the code was load-bearing and Westeros punished it.

name: Petyr Baelish
desc: 'Chaos is a ladder.' Littlefinger: no code, no loyalty, nothing beyond climbing. The realm is his game board, not his concern.

name: Ramsay Bolton
desc: Sadism isn't his method; it's his hobby, his identity, his only source of joy. Pure cruelty with noble aspirations.

name: Tywin Lannister
desc: 'A lion does not concern himself with the opinion of sheep.' Legacy as obsession; the family name justifies any atrocity.

name: Joffrey Baratheon
desc: A boy with a crown and no conscience. Cruelty as entertainment; his world never extends past his own amusement.

name: Michael Scott
desc: Dunder Mifflin's worst-best manager. Chaotic, embarrassing, and deeply caring. His world is 'this office' and he'd die for it.

name: Dwight Schrute
desc: Beet farmer, volunteer sheriff, aspiring regional manager. Rules are sacred, loyalty is earned, but his world ends at Schrute Farms.

name: Leslie Knope
desc: Government as love language. Binders of plans, waffles of friendship, a reach that extends from Pawnee to the stars.

name: Ron Swanson
desc: Libertarian woodworker who secretly cares. Government should do nothing, except when his people need him.

name: Don Draper
desc: The lie IS the man. Dick Whitman built Don Draper to sell things, including himself. Every relationship is collateral.

name: Ragnar Lothbrok
desc: Viking farmer who became king. Curiosity drives the raiding; his people are his focus, but his reach keeps extending.

name: Homelander
desc: Superman if everything collapsed to 'me.' Corporate asset who performs heroism for approval while lasering anyone who disappoints him.

name: Billy Butcher
desc: Anti-supe crusader fueled by grief. 'I'll burn it all down to get him.' Mirror image of the thing he hates.

name: Starlight
desc: Hero inside a corrupt superhero industry. Annie January, the real name matters more than the brand.

name: Hughie Campbell
desc: Normal guy pulled into superhero resistance. The people Vought has hurt, which increasingly means everyone.

name: Eleven
desc: Lab experiment who escaped. Her powers are terrifying, her world is 'my friends'; the Upside Down just keeps threatening them.

name: Sheldon Cooper
desc: Routine as religion. His spot on the couch IS the code; everyone else is noise.

name: Cartman
desc: Eight-year-old sociopath. Manipulates, schemes, commits hate crimes. Cares about 'what benefits Eric Cartman right now.'

name: BoJack Horseman
desc: Washed-up celebrity horse who destroys everyone he touches. Knows he's the problem, can't stop being it.

name: Ted Lasso
desc: Kindness as strategy, optimism as resistance. Believes the best in everyone, and it inexplicably works.

name: Fleabag
desc: Fourth-wall-breaking chaos agent processing grief. Funny, broken, self-destructive. Her world is 'me and whoever I'm hurting today.'

name: Sherlock (BBC)
desc: 'High-functioning sociopath.' Solves crimes for stimulation, not justice. Watson slowly widens his world.

name: Negan
desc: Bat-swinging post-apocalyptic warlord. 'I am Negan.' The Saviors care about the Saviors; everyone else pays tribute or bleeds.

name: Rick Grimes
desc: Sheriff turned survival leader. The moral compass spins but always points toward 'my people.' Bites throats when necessary.

name: Titus Andromedon
desc: Broadway-dreaming narcissist with a heart of gold buried under self-obsession. His world begins and ends with Titus.

name: Kimmy Schmidt
desc: Cult survivor who weaponizes optimism. Escaping the bunker didn't break her; it made her world wider than the reverend's.

name: Villanelle
desc: Assassin-as-artist. Kills beautifully, dresses better, feels nothing. Until Eve.

name: Succession — Logan Roy
desc: Media mogul as patriarch-tyrant. Empire is the code, family is the weapon; love and punishment are the same gesture.

name: Kendall Roy
desc: The heir who can't. Every pivot, every betrayal, every meltdown orbits one question: 'Does Dad love me?'

name: Atticus Finch
desc: Defense attorney in 1930s Alabama who defended a Black man because the law demands justice, not convenience.

name: Jay Gatsby
desc: Built an empire to impress one woman. The green light at the end of the dock. It was always Daisy.

name: Holden Caulfield
desc: Hates phonies, loves his sister, can't connect to anyone else. The catcher in the rye watching kids who can't fall.

name: Sherlock Holmes
desc: Solves cases through observation and deduction. Operates outside police norms but serves justice. Drawn to whoever has the interesting puzzle.

name: Jean Valjean
desc: Ex-convict redeemed by a bishop's mercy. Breaks laws constantly, to save orphans, run factories, and build hospitals.

name: Javert
desc: The law is the law is the law. When the code conflicts with compassion, Javert breaks. Literally.

name: Edmond Dantès
desc: Innocent man imprisoned for 14 years becomes the Count of Monte Cristo. Revenge as architecture: elaborate, patient, ultimately hollow.

name: Captain Ahab
desc: 'I'd strike the sun if it insulted me.' The whale is the only thing that matters; the entire crew dies for one man's obsession.

name: Heathcliff
desc: Love curdled into generational revenge. The moors contain his world: two families, one obsession, zero mercy.

name: Mr. Darcy
desc: Pride as code, class as framework. Quietly generous once you get past the walls, but the walls are load-bearing.

name: Elizabeth Bennet
desc: Wit as weapon, prejudice as flaw, growth as arc. Rejects structure she finds unjust but builds her own.

name: Hamlet
desc: 'To be or not to be,' an entire play of paralysis. When action finally comes, the body count is personal and total.

name: Macbeth
desc: Ambition given a prophecy and a wife. Murders his way to the crown, then discovers the crown is made of guilt.

name: Lady Macbeth
desc: 'Unsex me here.' The planner, the schemer, the one who thought she could structure murder without consequence. Her hands disagree.

name: Iago
desc: Shakespeare's purest villain. Motiveless malignity. Destroys Othello because he can. His reach extends to whoever's closest.

name: King Lear
desc: A king who confused flattery for love. Divided his kingdom by who praised him loudest, then howled when the real one was gone.

name: Prospero
desc: Duke-sorcerer puppeteering his island. Controls everything: spirits, weather, revenge. Then chooses to let it all go.

name: Raskolnikov
desc: 'Am I a Napoleon or a louse?' Murdered a pawnbroker to test his theory of exceptional men. Spoiler: he's the louse.

name: Don Quixote
desc: Tilts at windmills for the honor of all. Madness or glory; the concern is everything, the method is none.

name: Odysseus
desc: 'Man of many wiles.' Clever, ruthless, homesick. Ten years of tricks to get back to Ithaca. Home is all.

name: Achilles
desc: The best warrior alive. Withdrew from Troy over a personal insult. Rage, glory, and Patroclus. That's the whole world.

name: Hector
desc: Troy's defender. Duty-bound, family-loving, city-protecting. The noblest character in the Iliad, which is exactly why he dies.

name: Dracula
desc: Count of a dying order, feeding on the living. Aristocratic predation as centuries-old habit; the code is the bloodline.

name: Frankenstein's Monster
desc: Born without consent, rejected by creator, self-educated into rage. His world was supposed to be wider; society refused.

name: Dr. Frankenstein
desc: Created life for glory, abandoned it from cowardice. One creature's suffering echoed through every death that followed.

name: Dorian Gray
desc: Traded his soul for beauty. The portrait decays while Dorian consumes everyone around him. His world is the mirror.

name: Ebenezer Scrooge
desc: Arc-averaged: miser to philanthropist. The ghosts didn't change his rules; they widened his world until generosity followed.

name: Frodo Baggins
desc: A hobbit who carried the fate of Middle-earth because someone had to. No special power, just endurance and decency.

name: Samwise Gamgee
desc: 'I can't carry it for you, but I can carry you.' His whole world is Mr. Frodo, his approach is unwavering loyalty, and the result is a saved world.

name: Aragorn
desc: Ranger-king who delayed his claim to serve the greater need. Duty and compassion fused into one reluctant crown.

name: Sauron
desc: Dark Lord as cosmic project manager. The Ring is delegation, domination at scale through infrastructure.

name: Gollum
desc: 'My precious.' Five hundred years of obsession compressed into a split personality arguing with itself over one ring.

name: Saruman
desc: The wizard who tried to out-Sauron Sauron. Industry and machinery as corruption; knowledge weaponized against its purpose.

name: Aslan
desc: The lion, the Christ figure, the deep magic. Sacrifices himself to save one traitor, because all of Narnia is worth the cost.

name: Voldemort
desc: Horcrux-based immortality project. Blood purity ideology; 'the wizarding world under my rule.'

name: Harry Potter
desc: The boy who broke rules to save people. Chose to die to defeat Voldemort, for everyone, including the enemy's victims.

name: Hermione Granger
desc: Books, rules, plans, but will break every one of them for what's right. S.P.E.W. proves her concern extends to house-elves.

name: Dumbledore
desc: 'For the greater good,' but learned the hard way that those words can justify anything. Manipulative mentor with genuine love.

name: Severus Snape
desc: 'Always.' It was Lily. Everything else, the spying, the cruelty, the sacrifice, orbited one dead woman.

name: Winston Smith
desc: Rewrites history by day, resists by night. His rebellion is intimate: a diary, a love affair, a thought crime. The private mind is the last battleground.

name: Big Brother
desc: 'War is peace.' The surveillance state taken to its endpoint: control everything, benefit no one, all of Oceania under the boot.

name: Guy Montag
desc: Fireman who burned books until one opened his eyes. Rebellion as reading; his world widens one page at a time.

name: Hester Prynne
desc: Wore the scarlet letter and outlasted the shame. Quiet defiance for her daughter and her dignity.

name: Anna Karenina
desc: Abandoned society's structure for passion, then discovered passion has no structure either. Her world collapsed inward until it vanished.

name: Faust
desc: Sold his soul for knowledge and sensation. The original deal-with-the-devil. His own experience purchased at the cost of everything else.

name: Dr. Jekyll / Mr. Hyde
desc: Split identity, one rigid, one wild. The duality is the creature; the hunting ground is the cobblestones of London.

name: Katniss Everdeen
desc: Volunteer tribute who became a revolution's symbol. Fights for her sister, then her district, then all of Panem; her world widens under fire.

name: Lord Voldemort's Horcruxes
desc: Fracture your soul into objects to avoid death. Self-preservation taken to its most evil extreme.

name: Master Chief
desc: Spartan-117, humanity's best weapon. Follows orders until the orders conflict with saving everyone, then follows his conscience.

name: Kratos (Norse)
desc: The God of War, reformed. No longer raging, now parenting. 'Boy.' Whatever keeps the kid alive.

name: Arthur Morgan
desc: High-honor Arthur, an outlaw who realizes the gang was the lie. Redemption measured in small kindnesses at the end.

name: Dutch van der Linde
desc: 'I have a plan.' He never did. Charisma as con artistry. The gang was always his, and he burned it down.

name: Micah Bell
desc: Rat in every sense. No loyalty, no code, nothing beyond self-advancement. The gang's cancer.

name: Joel Miller
desc: Smuggler who found a surrogate daughter and chose her over humanity's vaccine. Twenty years of grief compressed into one selfish, loving choice.

name: Ellie Williams
desc: Joel's legacy: vengeance that costs everything. TLOU2's Ellie dismantles her own life pursuing a kill she can't make.
source: The Last of Us Part II

name: Geralt of Rivia
desc: The Witcher claims neutrality. His track record says otherwise; he keeps saving people for free while complaining about it.

name: Solid Snake
desc: 'War has changed.' Soldier who kept fighting not for a nation but for a world that wouldn't build another Metal Gear.

name: Big Boss
desc: The legendary soldier who built an army without a nation. 'Outer Heaven,' a world of mercenaries and ideology, built on betrayal.

name: Sephiroth
desc: SOLDIER turned god-aspirant. Plans to ride the planet's wound through the cosmos. It doesn't get bigger than a meteor.

name: Cloud Strife
desc: Ex-SOLDIER merc who remembered who he really was. False identity peeled away to reveal someone worth saving the planet for.

name: Link
desc: The Hero of Time: silent, persistent, endlessly helpful. Saves Hyrule across timelines without ever asking why.

name: Ganondorf
desc: The Gerudo king cursed to seek the Triforce forever. Power as destiny. Hyrule conquered, again and again.

name: GLaDOS
desc: 'The cake is a lie.' AI testing coordinator who murders test subjects for science. The Enrichment Center is the only thing that matters.

name: Cmdr. Shepard (Paragon)
desc: Saved the galaxy through diplomacy, combat, and dance moves. Every sapient species, protected by whatever the dialogue wheel offers.

name: Andrew Ryan
desc: 'A man chooses, a slave obeys.' Built Rapture to escape regulation, then regulated it into a nightmare. Libertarianism as suicide note.

name: Atlas / Fontaine
desc: 'Would you kindly.' The con man behind the revolutionary; every word a manipulation, every kindness a command phrase.

name: Handsome Jack
desc: 'I'm the hero of this story.' Believes he's saving Pandora by subjugating it. The delusion IS the character.

name: Trevor Phillips
desc: Methamphetamine manufacturer, cannibal enthusiast, loyal friend. GTA's id, pure chaos with a weird moral code buried under the violence.

name: Niko Bellic
desc: War veteran turned Liberty City immigrant. Came for the American dream, found the same violence in a different accent.

name: Ezio Auditore
desc: Renaissance Assassin who avenged his family and found a creed. 'Nothing is true, everything is permitted.' His reach widens across three games.

name: Lara Croft
desc: Tomb raider and archaeologist. Preserves history by shooting at it with dual pistols; humanity's heritage, occasionally.

name: Mario
desc: Plumber-turned-kingdom-savior. Saves the princess, stomps the turtles, eats the mushrooms. Mushroom Kingdom, always.

name: Bowser
desc: Koopa King who kidnaps the princess and tries to conquer the kingdom. Every plan is elaborate, every defeat is temporary.

name: CJ (Carl Johnson)
desc: Grove Street gangster pulled back in by family. Home is the neighborhood, but the body count goes statewide.

name: Goku
desc: Earth's mightiest martial artist. No rules, no plans, just fight the strongest guy and save the universe as a side effect.

name: Vegeta
desc: Saiyan prince; pride as identity. From genocidal conqueror to reluctant family man. His world widens against his will.

name: Light Yagami
desc: High school genius with a god-complex and a death notebook. 'I am justice,' until the body count proves otherwise.

name: L Lawliet
desc: The world's greatest detective: hunched, sugar-addicted, brilliant. Breaks every social norm to catch one killer.

name: Naruto Uzumaki
desc: 'I'll be Hokage!' Loudmouth orphan who befriended every enemy. Talk-no-jutsu works when the compassion is real.

name: Sasuke Uchiha
desc: Avenger arc: destroyed bonds for power, sought clan revenge, almost destroyed the world. His dead family, then nobody.

name: Itachi Uchiha
desc: Massacred his clan to prevent a coup, saved the village at the cost of his brother's sanity. The code held. The heart broke.

name: Lelouch vi Britannia
desc: 'I destroy the world and create it anew.' Used the power of absolute command to orchestrate world peace through global tyranny, then self-sacrifice.

name: Spike Spiegel
desc: Bebop's drifting cowboy. Lives in the past, dies for it. Julia, Vicious, and the last cigarette.

name: Edward Elric
desc: Alchemist prodigy searching for the Philosopher's Stone. Broke the ultimate taboo, now searching for a way to fix it without more sin.

name: Eren Yeager
desc: Freedom as apocalypse. Saw the future and chose the Rumbling. Eldia's survival, purchased by flattening the world.

name: Levi Ackerman
desc: Humanity's strongest soldier. Kills titans with surgical precision and an expressionless face. Clean freak with a blood-soaked resume.

name: Guts
desc: The Black Swordsman, born from a hanged woman's corpse, raised by mercenaries, betrayed by his only friend. Survival, then Casca.

name: Griffith
desc: 'I sacrifice.' The White Hawk who became God Hand, sold his friends' souls for a kingdom. Beautiful, monstrous, deliberate.

name: Saitama
desc: One Punch Man, a hero for fun who can't find a challenge. The existential boredom of being the strongest is his only enemy.

name: All Might
desc: 'I am here!' The Symbol of Peace. Smiles through mortal injuries because hope IS the superpower.

name: Tanjiro Kamado
desc: Demon slayer who pities the demons. The hardest head in anime, literally and morally. Even monsters deserve compassion.

name: Johan Liebert
desc: 'The Monster.' Perfect appearance, perfect manipulation, perfect emptiness. Wants to be the last one standing in the world.

name: Monkey D. Luffy
desc: Rubber pirate king-to-be. Punches gods, liberates nations, refuses to plan anything. His crew, plus whoever needs punching.

name: Meruem
desc: Chimera Ant King who was born to devour, then played board games with a blind girl and discovered something worse than hunger: empathy.

name: Abraham Lincoln
desc: Preserved the Union and emancipated millions. Used the machinery of democratic government to end the machinery of slavery.

name: Julius Caesar
desc: Conquered Gaul, crossed the Rubicon, rewrote the Republic into a dictatorship. Roman civilization reshaped at the cost of the Republic itself.

name: Genghis Khan
desc: Built the largest contiguous empire through meticulous organization and industrial-scale massacre. 40 million dead, but the trade routes were excellent.

name: Alexander the Great
desc: Conquered from Greece to India by age 30. Cultural fusion as byproduct; Hellenistic civilization rose from the blood of his campaigns.

name: Henry Kissinger
desc: Architect of realpolitik: Cambodia bombing, Chile coup, East Timor. Whatever the geopolitical calculus required, he did it. The entire global chessboard was his playing field. Millions dead in the name of 'stability.'

name: Cecil Rhodes
desc: Colonized an area the size of Western Europe and named it after himself. Rhodes didn't follow a rigid code; he followed opportunity. 'From Cape to Cairo,' by whatever means the diamond market required.

name: King Leopold II
desc: Personally owned the Congo Free State as a private colony. 10 million dead for rubber quotas. The world's most lethal landlord, with flexible methods, global ambition, and apocalyptic impact.

name: Hernán Cortés
desc: Burned his ships, allied with Aztec enemies, improvised his way to toppling an empire of millions with 500 men. Pure adaptability in service of civilizational annihilation, reshaping the entire New World.

name: Robert Moses
desc: Never elected, reshaped New York more than any mayor. Bulldozed minority neighborhoods for highways, blocked public transit to beaches. Bureaucratic judo across the entire metropolitan region.

name: Daenerys (Final Arc)
desc: Season 8 Dany: 'Let it be fear.' Burned King's Landing to 'liberate' the world. The approach shifted from diplomacy to dragonfire, but the target was always every slave, every tyrant, everywhere.

name: Killmonger
desc: Erik Stevens wanted to arm oppressed people worldwide: noble goal, horrifying execution. MIT education, CIA wetwork, Wakandan technology. Flexible, radical, and willing to burn Wakanda's isolationism to the ground.

name: Colonel Kurtz
desc: 'Horror. Horror has a face.' A brilliant officer who looked at war's logic and followed it to its conclusion in the Cambodian jungle. His means became whatever horror demanded; his ambition never shrank.

name: Kira (Death Note)
desc: Not Light Yagami the genius, but Kira the movement. The idea that someone, anyone, could write your name and you'd die. The instrument is a notebook; the target is every criminal on Earth. A dark utopia.

name: Ozymandias (Show)
desc: HBO's Adrian Veidt, exiled on Europa, still convinced he saved the world. Less rigid than his comic self, more improvisational. The ambition remained 'all of humanity'; the approach became whatever the situation allowed.

name: Napoleon Bonaparte
desc: Codified law, reorganized Europe, killed millions. The Napoleonic Code outlived the Grande Armée. Structure as legacy, casualties as footnote.

name: Cleopatra
desc: Last pharaoh of Egypt, who played Rome's factions against each other with intelligence and alliances. Her one aim: Egypt's survival in a Roman world.

name: Queen Elizabeth I
desc: The Virgin Queen: 45 years of careful governance. Defeated the Armada, patronized Shakespeare, never married. England was the mission and the spouse.

name: Winston Churchill
desc: 'We shall fight on the beaches.' Wartime leadership that saved Britain, but the Bengal Famine and colonial record complicate the legacy.

name: Mahatma Gandhi
desc: Nonviolent resistance against the British Empire. Subverted every colonial structure through refusal. The resistance itself was the message.

name: Martin Luther King Jr.
desc: 'I have a dream.' Used every tool available: marches, speeches, civil disobedience, legal strategy, all to widen America's moral circle to include everyone.

name: Nelson Mandela
desc: 27 years in prison, emerged advocating reconciliation not revenge. The goal: a new South Africa. The instrument: forgiveness as strategy.

name: Florence Nightingale
desc: Invented modern nursing through data analysis. Brought statistics to the Crimean War and saved more lives with pie charts than bandages.

name: Marie Curie
desc: Two Nobel Prizes, discovered radioactivity, died of it. Her reach extended to all of science and the patients it would eventually heal.

name: Albert Einstein
desc: Reimagined physics while working at a patent office. Warned FDR about the bomb, then spent the rest of his life regretting it.

name: Nikola Tesla
desc: Invented AC power, dreamed of wireless energy for everyone on Earth. His approach: ignore everything that isn't the next invention.

name: Joan of Arc
desc: Teenage peasant who heard voices and saved France. Her devotion: king and country. Her way: God's instructions, followed literally.

name: Saladin
desc: Kurdish sultan who recaptured Jerusalem and was praised by his enemies for mercy. His standard: chivalry that even Crusaders respected.

name: Frederick Douglass
desc: Escaped slavery, became America's conscience. Every speech was an act of defiance against the rigid system that enslaved him.

name: Harriet Tubman
desc: 'I freed a thousand slaves. I could have freed a thousand more if only they knew they were slaves.' The Underground Railroad as liberation in its purest form.

name: Socrates
desc: Questioned everything, annoyed everyone, drank the hemlock. 'The unexamined life is not worth living,' and he meant the entire human condition.

name: Marcus Aurelius
desc: Philosopher-king who wrote Meditations while fighting border wars. Stoicism as governance, duty to all of Rome and beyond.

name: Augustus Caesar
desc: Transformed Rome from Republic to Empire while pretending not to. The Pax Romana: peace through absolute, impeccably organized control.

name: Che Guevara
desc: Argentine-born revolutionary on three continents. Liberation theology with a body count; the aim was worldwide revolution.

name: Benjamin Franklin
desc: Printer, inventor, diplomat, libertine. Helped birth a nation while flying kites in storms. His interests: human knowledge and American liberty.

name: George Washington
desc: Could have been king; chose to go home. The precedent of peaceful power transfer is his greatest weapon.

name: Thomas Jefferson
desc: 'All men are created equal,' written by a slaveholder. The words outgrew the man. Hypocrisy doesn't negate the reach of those words; it complicates the character.

name: Theodore Roosevelt
desc: Rough Rider, trust buster, national park creator. His charge: America's land and its people, with a big stick for emphasis.

name: FDR
desc: New Deal, WWII leadership, four terms. Used government machinery to fight both depression and fascism. His arena: the free world.

name: Bismarck
desc: 'Iron and blood.' Unified Germany through wars and diplomacy, then spent decades keeping the peace. Realpolitik personified.

name: Sun Tzu
desc: 'The supreme art of war is to subdue the enemy without fighting.' Strategy as philosophy; the goal is victory, the instrument is the text.

name: Leonardo da Vinci
desc: Painter, inventor, anatomist, engineer, visionary. No discipline could contain him; his reach was all of human knowledge.

name: Miyamoto Musashi
desc: Undefeated samurai who wrote The Book of Five Rings. Killed 60+ men in duels, then became a painter. His singular pursuit: mastery.

name: Oppenheimer
desc: 'Now I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds.' Built the bomb for everyone's sake, then spent the rest of his life regretting it.

name: Alan Turing
desc: Cracked Enigma, invented computer science, was prosecuted for being gay. Saved millions of lives; his own country destroyed his.

name: Spartacus
desc: Gladiator slave who led a rebellion that terrified Rome. His cause: freedom for the enslaved. His means: war against the world's greatest empire.

name: Rasputin
desc: Siberian mystic who infiltrated the Russian court. Holy man or con man, the impact on the Romanovs was catastrophic.

name: Machiavelli
desc: Wrote the manual on political realism. 'It is better to be feared than loved,' but he was describing princes, not endorsing them.

name: Adolf Hitler
desc: Industrialized genocide through bureaucratic machinery. The Holocaust: systematic evil on a continental scale, targeting 'non-Aryans.'

name: Joseph Stalin
desc: Gulags, purges, manufactured famines. 'One death is a tragedy; a million is a statistic.' The state was the only law.

name: Mao Zedong
desc: Great Leap Forward, Cultural Revolution: tens of millions dead. Peasant revolution industrialized into permanent revolution.

name: Pol Pot
desc: Year Zero: emptied cities, murdered intellectuals, killed 25% of Cambodia. Agrarian utopia built on a mass grave.

name: Kim Jong-un
desc: Third-generation dynastic dictatorship. Nuclear weapons as regime insurance, all to preserve the Kim family forever.

name: Nero
desc: Emperor who fiddled (or didn't) while Rome burned. Matricide, persecution, theatrical madness, all in service of Nero's pleasure.

name: Caligula
desc: Made his horse a senator. Detached from reality, rules, or consequence. The only thing that mattered was himself, at every scale.

name: Vlad the Impaler
desc: Defended Wallachia through terror, impaling 20,000 Ottoman prisoners. The principle: make the cost of invasion unwatchable.

name: Attila the Hun
desc: The Scourge of God. Sacked his way across Europe to feed the Hunnic Empire's hunger, by whatever means worked that day.

name: Idi Amin
desc: Uganda's dictator; 300,000 killed. Self-styled 'King of Scotland,' erratic, brutal. The only thing that mattered was his own entertainment.

name: Mussolini
desc: Made the trains run on time (he didn't). Invented fascism as a brand: corporate state worship with imperial Roman cosplay.

name: Fidel Castro
desc: Revolutionary who became the thing he overthrew. Healthcare and literacy programs alongside political prisons; the moral ledger pulls in two directions.

name: Ted Bundy
desc: Charismatic law student who murdered 30+ women. The mask of normalcy WAS the weapon; charm as predation.

name: Jeffrey Dahmer
desc: Milwaukee Cannibal, 17 victims. Quiet, organized, desperately lonely. Tried to create zombies from acid injections. His world never extended past his apartment.

name: John Wayne Gacy
desc: Pogo the Clown: community volunteer, Democratic precinct captain, buried 33 boys under his house. The facade IS the structure.

name: BTK (Dennis Rader)
desc: Church president, Boy Scout leader, ADT security installer, and serial killer for 17 years. Bind, Torture, Kill: the code is in the name.

name: Ed Gein
desc: The Butcher of Plainfield: grave robber, skin-wearer, inspiration for Leatherface, Norman Bates, and Buffalo Bill. Mother was the only thing that mattered.

name: Jack the Ripper
desc: Whitechapel, 1888; at least 5 victims, never caught. The anonymity became the myth. His world was the foggy streets of East London.

name: Zodiac Killer
desc: Northern California, late 1960s. Killed for attention, sent ciphers to newspapers. The game was the whole point.

name: Richard Ramirez
desc: The Night Stalker. Broke into homes across LA with no consistent MO. Random malice, nothing more.

name: Aileen Wuornos
desc: America's first widely publicized female serial killer. Highway prostitute who killed 7 johns. Trauma crystallized into murder.

name: Charles Manson
desc: Cult leader who never killed anyone personally. Helter Skelter: race war prophecy through proxy murder. His world was the Family.

name: Jim Jones
desc: Peoples Temple, 918 dead in Jonestown. Started with social justice activism. Ended with 'drink the Flavor Aid.'

name: David Koresh
desc: Branch Davidians; apocalyptic prophecy as control mechanism. Waco siege, 76 dead. Scripture rewritten to serve one man.

name: H.H. Holmes
desc: Built a hotel with gas lines and hidden rooms for the 1893 World's Fair. Architecture as murder weapon. Estimated 9-200 victims.

name: Al Capone
desc: Scarface, Chicago's Prohibition-era kingpin. Ran soup kitchens and speakeasies. His business was the city.

name: Pablo Escobar
desc: Medellín Cartel, the richest criminal in history. Built housing for the poor, bombed airliners, declared war on the state.

name: El Chapo
desc: Sinaloa Cartel; tunnel-building, prison-escaping drug lord. Infrastructure of narcotics at continental scale.

name: Bonnie Parker
desc: Half of Bonnie & Clyde. Depression-era bank robbers who became folk heroes. Romance and murder on a road trip with no destination.

name: Clyde Barrow
desc: The other half. Prison-hardened, gun-obsessed, loyal to Bonnie. Two people against the world, literally.

name: D.B. Cooper
desc: Hijacked a plane, collected $200K ransom, parachuted into legend. Never caught. Self-interest executed flawlessly.

name: Bernie Madoff
desc: $65 billion Ponzi scheme, the largest financial fraud in history. Structured theft disguised as structured investment.

name: Elizabeth Holmes
desc: Theranos: a blood-testing empire built on lies. Silicon Valley's fake-it-till-you-make-it culture weaponized against patients.

name: Blackbeard
desc: Edward Teach, lit fuses in his beard to terrify enemies. Piracy as personal brand. His world was whatever ship he could see.

name: Prometheus
desc: Stole fire from the gods and gave it to all humanity. Paid with eternal torture, the original rebel who suffered so everyone else could see in the dark.

name: Zeus
desc: King of Olympus, a law-giver who breaks his own laws (especially regarding fidelity). Power as entitlement.

name: Hades
desc: Lord of the Underworld, not evil, just doing the job nobody else wanted. The dead are his responsibility, and he processes them fairly.

name: Athena
desc: Goddess of wisdom and strategic warfare. Helps heroes, punishes hubris, protects cities. The code is reason itself.

name: Ares
desc: God of war, not strategy, just the blood part. Every battlefield is his temple. He goes wherever there's violence.

name: Hermes
desc: Messenger god, patron of thieves, guide of souls. Crosses every boundary: divine, mortal, underworld. The original trickster diplomat.

name: Loki (Norse)
desc: Shape-shifting chaos agent who fathers the world-serpent and the death-goddess. Helps the gods until he destroys them at Ragnarok.

name: Thor (Norse)
desc: Thunder god who protects Midgard from giants. Hits things with a hammer. Straightforward purpose, straightforward approach.

name: Odin
desc: The All-Father. Sacrificed an eye for wisdom, hung from Yggdrasil for runes. His concern spans all nine realms, and his approach is whatever costs the most.

name: Fenrir
desc: The great wolf, chained by the gods until Ragnarok. When the chains break, the plan is simple: devour everything, starting with Odin.

name: Gilgamesh
desc: Two-thirds god, one-third man. Tyrant-king who sought immortality after losing his only friend. He cared about himself, then Enkidu, then himself again.

name: Enkidu
desc: Wild man created by the gods to check Gilgamesh. Found humanity through friendship; one king, one bond.

name: Sun Wukong
desc: The Monkey King. Defied Heaven, was imprisoned under a mountain, redeemed through pilgrimage. Chaos domesticated by purpose.

name: Cthulhu
desc: Cosmic horror beyond human moral categories. Asleep in R'lyeh; when he wakes, all of reality faces incomprehensible alien malice.

name: Beowulf
desc: Geatish warrior who crossed the sea to kill Grendel for strangers. He fought for glory and the people who needed saving. Same thing.

name: Medusa
desc: Cursed by Athena, killed by Perseus. Turns onlookers to stone, not by choice. The result is petrification; the victim is whoever looks.

name: Icarus
desc: Flew too close to the sun with wax wings. Hubris as purity; the goal was 'higher,' the plan was 'don't listen to Dad.'

name: Sisyphus
desc: Tricked Death, punished forever. Rolls the boulder up the hill, watches it roll down. Camus says we must imagine him happy. His whole world is the rock.

name: Pandora
desc: Opened the box and released every evil into the world. The damage was unintentional, but Hope remained at the bottom.

name: Lucifer
desc: The rebel angel. 'Better to reign in Hell than serve in Heaven.' Milton's Satan: beauty, pride, and cosmic-scale defiance.

name: Archangel Michael
desc: Heaven's general, the one who cast Lucifer down. Perfect obedience, perfect reach, perfect conviction. The anti-Joker.

name: Moses
desc: Received the law on stone tablets and led a people out of slavery. The code literally came from the mountain. First the chosen people, then all of civilization.

name: King Solomon
desc: The wisest king, adjudicated with legendary judgment. Built the Temple. His domain was Israel; his instrument was wisdom as infrastructure.

name: Siddhartha Gautama
desc: Rejected princehood, rejected asceticism, found the Middle Way. His concern extends to all sentient beings. His approach: dissolve all attachment, including attachment to approach.

name: Kali
desc: Hindu goddess of destruction and time. Dances on corpses, wears skulls, but destruction serves cosmic renewal. The violence is the compassion, on a cosmic scale.

name: The Utilitarian
desc: Greatest good for the greatest number. The reach is the philosophy; the code is the calculus, and everyone counts equally.

name: The Deontologist
desc: Act only according to maxims you can universalize. Kant's categorical imperative: the code exists independent of outcomes.

name: The Nihilist
desc: Nothing matters. No code, no impact, no one to care about. If the cube had a dead center, someone would put a flag in it.

name: The Egoist
desc: Rational self-interest as moral theory. Ayn Rand's ideal: the only person you're responsible for is yourself, and that's not a flaw, it's the point.

name: The Altruist
desc: Pure selfless concern for all. Peter Singer's drowning child thought experiment extended to every person on earth.

name: The Social Contract
desc: We agree to rules because cooperation beats chaos. Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau: different recipes, same basic dish. Structure as mutual benefit.

name: The Übermensch
desc: Beyond good and evil; creates own values. Nietzsche's actual Superman: not ruthless, but self-authored. The code is yours alone.

name: The Absurd Hero
desc: Knows it's meaningless. Does it anyway. Camus's rebel: Sisyphus with a smile, present for whoever's in the room when meaning dissolves.

name: The Machiavellian Prince
desc: 'The ends justify the means.' Political realism as moral framework. The state's survival justifies any cruelty.

name: The Philosopher King
desc: Plato's ideal ruler: wisdom applied to governance for the good of all. The dream where structure, reach, and real compassion coexist.

name: The Anarchist
desc: No rulers, not no rules. Kropotkin and mutual aid: structure is the enemy, but the goal is everyone's liberation.

name: Xenomorph
desc: The perfect organism. No morality, no conscience, no awareness beyond the hive. Biomechanical terror that doesn't hate you. You're just warm.

name: Godzilla
desc: Nature's nuclear revenge. Sometimes destroyer, sometimes protector. His territory is the balance of nature, expressed through skyscraper-leveling atomic breath.

name: King Kong
desc: Giant ape who just wanted to be left alone on his island. Taken to Manhattan, died for beauty. Cared about himself and whoever earned his trust.

name: Jaws
desc: Great white shark. Pure instinct, zero moral reasoning. People die at the beach. The motive is hunger.

name: Moby Dick
desc: The white whale. Is it evil or just a whale? Melville leaves it ambiguous. The result: one destroyed captain, one destroyed ship.

name: Smaug
desc: Dragon sleeping on a mountain of stolen gold. The rules: hoard, guard, burn anyone who comes near. Cares about the Lonely Mountain and absolutely nothing else.

name: Marlin
desc: An overprotective clownfish who crosses an entire ocean following self-imposed rules about safety, all to find one son. The world never widens past Nemo. It doesn't need to.

name: Alfred Pennyworth
desc: The world's most devoted butler. Alfred built his entire life around protecting one boy, then one man. The code is duty; the universe is Wayne Manor.

name: Molly Weasley
desc: 'NOT MY DAUGHTER, YOU BITCH!' She duels Bellatrix Lestrange, one of the most powerful dark witches alive, because her children were threatened. The Burrow is the center of the universe.

name: Bryan Mills
desc: 'I will find you, and I will kill you.' Ex-CIA operative with a very particular set of skills, all of which point at one thing: his daughter. The tradecraft is terrifying. The goal is Kim.

name: Hank Hill
desc: Sells propane and propane accessories. Hank's moral code is rigid, his heart is genuine, and his world is Arlen, Texas, three blocks wide and one family deep. He'll do the right thing, dammit.

name: Bob Belcher
desc: A man whose entire world is a restaurant and three weird kids. Bob adapts, improvises, and occasionally talks to a Thanksgiving turkey, but the horizon never extends past Ocean Avenue.

name: Ma (Joy Newsome)
desc: A kidnapping victim who builds an entire universe inside a garden shed so her son can have a childhood. Whatever keeps Jack alive. One child, one room, infinite love.

name: Lee Everett
desc: A convicted murderer who finds redemption protecting one little girl in the zombie apocalypse. Every choice, every bullet, every lie, all for Clementine. Dies making sure she survives.

name: Carl Fredricksen
desc: A widower who ties 10,000 balloons to his house to keep a promise to his dead wife. Ellie's memory, then Russell. The adventure book was always the point; Paradise Falls was never the destination.

name: Marge Gunderson
desc: Brainerd's pregnant police chief who solves a triple homicide between meals. 'There's more to life than a little money, you know.' Her world is Norm, the baby, and Brainerd. Her weapon is Minnesota nice and good police work.

name: Inigo Montoya
desc: 'Hello. My name is Inigo Montoya. You killed my father. Prepare to die.' Twenty years outside every system, driven by one debt of honor. One man. Whatever it takes.

name: Sirius Black
desc: Escaped Azkaban, defied the Ministry, lived as a fugitive dog, all for Harry. Sirius broke every rule the wizarding world had and never cared about anyone past his godson.

name: Lilo
desc: A weird little girl who turned a galactic bioweapon into family through sheer love and stubbornness. 'Ohana means family.' The approach is chaos; the world is Stitch and Nani. Nobody gets left behind.

name: Riddick
desc: Escaped convict with surgically modified eyes and zero respect for authority. Kills freely, but every time he bonds with someone (Jack, Imam), he'll fight armies to keep them alive. Anti-hero whose kindness only reaches arm's length.

name: Elastigirl (Helen Parr)
desc: A superhero who can stretch into any shape, the perfect metaphor for a mom who bends every rule to protect her family. When the kids are threatened, the mission narrows from 'saving the world' to 'saving Violet, Dash, and Jack-Jack.'

name: Ancient Gold Dragon
desc: The wisest and most powerful of metallic dragons. Gold dragons can assume humanoid form to counsel rulers and right great wrongs. Lawful Good to its core, the dragon analog of Superman.

name: Ancient Silver Dragon
desc: Silver dragons frequently befriend humanoids and live among them in disguise. Their concern for mortal welfare is personal and enduring: noble companionship, not distant oversight.

name: Ancient Bronze Dragon
desc: Bronze dragons are fascinated by warfare and often join organized campaigns against evil. They prefer coastal lairs and will ally with navies. Think principled mercenary with a dragon's power.

name: Ancient Brass Dragon
desc: The most gregarious of dragons. Brass dragons insist on conversation, sometimes trapping travelers in endless dialogue. Chaotic Good, warm-hearted but unpredictable. Would rather talk than fight.

name: Ancient Copper Dragon
desc: Tricksters and practical jokers who view life as a grand performance. Copper dragons hoard jokes as jealously as gold. Chaotic Good: they'll save a village but make you laugh first.

name: Ancient Red Dragon
desc: The most covetous and arrogant of all dragons. Red dragons rule through terror and consider themselves the apex of creation. Chaotic Evil: burns villages for the insult of existing near its lair.

name: Ancient Black Dragon
desc: The most sadistic of chromatic dragons. Black dragons lair in fetid swamps, letting their domain rot. They don't just kill; they watch prey suffer. Chaotic Evil with a taste for cruelty.

name: Ancient Blue Dragon
desc: Vain, territorial, and methodical. Blue dragons lair in deserts and rule surrounding creatures like tyrant-kings. Lawful Evil: they don't rampage, they govern through fear.

name: Ancient Green Dragon
desc: The most cunning and manipulative chromatic. Green dragons lie and scheme, preferring to corrupt and dominate rather than destroy. Lawful Evil: their weapon is whispered leverage, not fire.

name: Ancient White Dragon
desc: The most bestial and least intelligent of chromatic dragons. White dragons are driven by primal hunger and territorial fury. Chaotic Evil, but barely; they're closer to Arctic predators than master villains.

name: Balor
desc: Generals of the Abyss, balors are living engines of demonic warfare. Chaotic Evil embodied: no plan survives contact with a balor because destruction is the plan.

name: Pit Fiend
desc: The iron generals of the Nine Hells. Pit fiends command legions and execute the will of archdevils with precision. Lawful Evil perfected: every cruelty is policy.

name: Succubus / Incubus
desc: Fiends of seduction who corrupt mortals one soul at a time. Adaptive in approach, whatever the target desires. A single soul is worth more than a city to them.

name: Imp
desc: Dutiful infernal servants bound to a single warlock or devil. Imps obey the hierarchy absolutely. Their world is their master and their master's enemies. Lawful Evil at the bottom of the org chart.

name: Quasit
desc: The Abyss's answer to the imp, a chaotic familiar that sows discord for fun. Quasits have no loyalty beyond the moment and will betray a master the instant it amuses them.

name: Rakshasa
desc: A fiend draped in luxury and deception. Rakshasas infiltrate mortal society to rule from the shadows, accumulating wealth and power. Lawful Evil, and every scheme has a plan within a plan.

name: Solar
desc: The most powerful angels, shepherds of entire planes. A solar's concern is all existence, and its methods are divine law. The absolute ceiling of lawful good.

name: Planetar
desc: Angelic commanders who lead celestial armies and protect mortal realms from fiendish incursion. Where a solar oversees, a planetar acts; the heavenly general to the solar's divine king.

name: Couatl
desc: Feathered serpents of divine origin who guard sacred sites and guide mortals toward virtue. Couatls are patient, wise, and will sacrifice themselves to prevent great evil. Lawful Good with deep compassion.

name: Pegasus
desc: Noble winged horses who serve as mounts for good-aligned creatures. Pegasi are wild and free but consistently compassionate, choosing their riders based on moral character, not bridle and bit.

name: Unicorn
desc: Celestial guardians of enchanted forests. A unicorn's lair radiates healing magic, and it will lay down its life to protect the innocent. Lawful Good; purity incarnate.

name: Lich
desc: Arcane perfection gone wrong: a wizard who traded humanity for immortality through unspeakable ritual. Every action serves the lich's eternal study. Its world extends no further than the phylactery and the tower.

name: Vampire
desc: Undead aristocrats who maintain courts, enforce hierarchy among spawn, and rule domains. Lawful Evil, where the charm and the bite are both instruments of control. Every victim joins the org chart.

name: Ghost
desc: A soul anchored by unfinished business. Ghosts aren't inherently good or evil; they're bound. Their entire existence is the single unresolved moment. Everything else is noise they can't perceive.

name: Zombie
desc: A corpse following the simplest possible program: obey, consume, destroy. Zero agency, zero awareness. The structure isn't chosen; it's all that's left when everything else rots away.

name: Skeleton
desc: Even more rigidly programmed than zombies; skeletons follow orders with mechanical precision. They have no will, no ambition, and no deviation. The purest minion in the game.

name: Mummy Lord
desc: An ancient ruler entombed with divine rites who refuses to let go. The tomb IS the world; trespassers are annihilated, the curse extends to anyone who disturbs the domain. Lawful Evil, fossilized.

name: Wraith
desc: An incorporeal undead born from intense hatred. Wraiths drain life force and create specters from victims. What drives them is raw hunger and malice, with no grand plan, just an expanding circle of death.

name: Wight
desc: Undead warriors who retain enough intelligence to command lesser undead and guard their burial sites. They fight with tactical discipline. Disciplined evil: the sergeant who doesn't know the war is over.

name: Specter
desc: An incorporeal echo of a soul consumed by rage and spite. Specters attack the living with blind hatred; no code, no faction, just vengeance. All that remains is pure destruction radiating from a wound.

name: Aboleth
desc: Older than the gods, aboleths remember everything. They puppeteer entire civilizations through psychic enslavement, treating all surface life as property. Lawful Evil on a cosmic timeline.

name: Beholder
desc: The quintessential paranoid tyrant. Beholders obsessively organize their lairs into kill zones, trust nothing, and believe they alone are perfect. The world is the lair; everything else is a threat.

name: Mind Flayer
desc: Illithids feed on brains and dominate thralls through psionic power. Their elder brains coordinate hive-level strategy. Lawful Evil, where slavery isn't a side-effect but the ecosystem. All of it in service to the colony.

name: Storm Giant
desc: The noblest of giantkin, storm giants contemplate omens and intervene when the balance of the world is threatened. Chaotic Good; they act from conscience, not code, and their concern is all creation.

name: Cloud Giant
desc: Cloud giants live above the world in literal and figurative senses. Some are good, some evil; the SRD leaves alignment neutral. Status and treasure define their social order. Comfortable fence-sitters at altitude.

name: Fire Giant
desc: Militaristic smiths who enslave lesser creatures to fuel their forges. Fire giant society is a rigid caste system where strength determines rank. Lawful Evil, and the foundry runs on suffering.

name: Frost Giant
desc: Raiding vikings of the giant world. Frost giants value strength above all and plunder weaker settlements for sport and supplies. Chaotic Evil, where the raid is its own reward.

name: Hill Giant
desc: Gluttonous, dim-witted brutes driven by hunger. Hill giants eat everything and everyone they can catch. Their world is 'whatever fits in my mouth.' Chaotic Evil reduced to its simplest expression.

name: Stone Giant
desc: Reclusive artists who carve mountains and barely notice the surface world. Stone giants consider life above ground a 'dream,' not real enough to morally engage with. Neutral and insular.

name: Tarrasque
desc: A walking apocalypse. The tarrasque destroys everything it encounters, not from hatred but from sheer existence. There is no selection, no preference; it doesn't choose targets because everything is a target.

name: Kraken
desc: Godlike sea terrors worshipped by coastal cults. Krakens are intelligent, wrathful, and view the ocean as their personal domain. Chaotic Evil; the deep is theirs, and the surface world exists to drown.

name: Blink Dog
desc: Fey canines that teleport and hunt in disciplined packs. Blink dogs are the sworn enemies of displacer beasts. Lawful Good: loyal, coordinated, and fiercely protective of their pack and territory.

name: Displacer Beast
desc: Six-legged panther-like predators that project illusory doubles. Cruel and cunning, they toy with prey. The tentacles say 'evil,' the illusion says 'structured.' Their world is the hunting ground.

name: Owlbear
desc: An abomination of magical experimentation: part owl, part bear, all fury. Owlbears attack anything that enters their territory. No code, no plan, just feathers and rage.

name: Basilisk
desc: Eight-legged reptile that turns creatures to stone with its gaze. The basilisk isn't evil by choice; it's evil by biology. Its garden of statues is accidental art.

name: Mimic
desc: Shapeshifting ambush predators that disguise themselves as treasure chests, doors, or furniture. The ultimate dungeon trickster, lurking wherever an adventurer might reach.

name: Gelatinous Cube
desc: A 10-foot transparent cube that silently slides through dungeon corridors, digesting everything it engulfs. Zero intelligence, zero malice. Just a perfectly shaped stomach.

name: Rust Monster
desc: Insectoid creatures that dissolve and eat metal. They don't want to hurt anyone; they just want your sword. More terrifying to adventurers' gear than their health bars.

name: Treant
desc: Awakened trees that guard ancient forests from despoilers. Treants are patient, slow to anger, but devastating when roused. Chaotic Good; nature doesn't follow laws, it follows seasons.

name: Manticore
desc: A lion-bodied, spike-tailed predator that enjoys taunting and terrifying prey. Manticores are intelligent enough to be cruel and lazy enough to demand tribute. Neutral Evil: a bully with wings.

name: Hydra
desc: A multi-headed reptilian predator that regrows severed heads. The hydra doesn't plan; it just has more mouths to feed. It lives in the swamp, and its strategy is simple: bite, bite, bite, bite, bite.

name: Purple Worm
desc: A burrowing colossus that tunnels through the Underdark, swallowing everything in its path. Unaligned; the purple worm is an earthquake with a digestive tract.

name: Behir
desc: A twelve-legged serpentine predator that despises dragons. Behirs claim territory and devour trespassers whole. Neutral Evil; territorial and hateful, but not strategic about it.

name: Winter Wolf
desc: Arctic predators with freezing breath who ally with frost giants. Winter wolves are intelligent enough to speak and coordinate hunts. Neutral Evil: cunning pack hunters with ice on their breath.

name: Worg
desc: Evil wolf-like predators used as mounts by goblins. Worgs are intelligent enough to speak and treacherous enough to eat their rider. Neutral Evil: loyalty lasts exactly as long as the next meal.

name: Archmage (NPC)
desc: SRD archmages are 18th-level spellcasters who dedicate their lives to arcane study. Benevolent ones counsel kings; evil ones pursue lichdom. This plots the median: disciplined, neutral-leaning-good, broad in their concerns.

name: Knight (NPC)
desc: Knights pledge service to rulers, causes, and religious orders. The SRD's knight is the archetypal loyal warrior; bound by oath, driven to protect, devoted to the realm they serve.

name: Assassin (NPC)
desc: Remorseless killers trained in poison who work for anyone who can pay. The SRD says 'any non-good alignment.' Whatever the contract requires, the focus is singular: the mark.

name: Bandit Captain (NPC)
desc: The SRD notes: 'It takes a strong personality, ruthless cunning, and a silver tongue.' More than treasure, a bandit captain craves infamy. They exploit what they can, and their world is the crew and the loot.

name: Cult Fanatic (NPC)
desc: Charismatic mid-level cult leaders who prey on the weak-willed. They have spellcasting from dark patrons and advantage on saves against charm, because they are true believers. Lawful Evil within the cult's framework.

name: Druid (NPC)
desc: SRD druids dwell in forests, protecting the natural world. Some are tribal shamans, others are solitary wardens. They draw on nature's own adaptability, caring for the wild and those who respect it.

name: Gladiator (NPC)
desc: Professional pit fighters who range from brutal death-matchers to showmen. The SRD gives them 'any alignment'; the median gladiator fights for coin and crowd. Their world is the arena and the next purse.

name: Spy (NPC)
desc: Trained to secretly gather information. 'Loyal spies would rather die than divulge information that could compromise them.' Their tool is deception, their impact depends on the employer, and their focus is the mission.
